pi_items.source: setup_geoserver.sh line 137 creates workspace 'ne' via REST API
setup_geoserver.sh line 114 imports ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp as 'ne_countries' table; line 185 publishes as layer
EPSG registry (https://epsg.io/4326) - Official EPSG code for WGS 84 (World Geodetic System 1984), used in GPS
EPSG registry (https://epsg.io/3857) - Official EPSG code for Web Mercator / Pseudo-Mercator projection, standard for web mapping services (Google Maps, OpenStreetMap, Bing, ESRI)

pi_items.verified_value: Natural Earth v5.1.1 public domain dataset (https://www.naturalearthdata.com/downloads/110m-cultural-vectors/110m-admin-0-countries/)

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the Natural Earth public dataset (https://www.naturalearthdata.com/), specifically the 1:110m scale countries shapefile (ne_110m_admin_0_countries), a real, authoritative geospatial dataset containing country boundaries. The data is imported into PostGIS (version 3.3 with PostgreSQL 15) and published via GeoServer 2.25.2. The target layer is 'ne_countries' in the 'ne' workspace. The task requires restricting this layer to advertise ONLY two CRS (Coordinate Reference Systems): EPSG:4326 (WGS 84 - World Geodetic System used in GPS) and EPSG:3857 (Web Mercator - the standard projection for web mapping services). Both EPSG codes are official, authoritative codes from the EPSG registry.
